ja.intro: タクシーに　のりました。えきに　いきたいです。うんてんしゅに　なんといいますか。

options.text: えきまで　いきましょうか。
えきまで　おねがいします。
えきに　つきましたか。

ja.question: うんてんしゅに　なんといいますか。

tr.intro: Taksiye bindınız. İstasyona gitmek istiyorsunuz. Şoföre ne söylersiniz?
tr.question: Şoföre ne söylersiniz?

en.intro: You got in a taxi. You want to go to the station. What do you say to the driver?

options.text: Shall we go to the station?
Have we arrived at the station?

en.question: What do you say to the driver?

de.intro: Sie sind in ein Taxi eingestiegen. Sie möchten zum Bahnhof. Was sagen Sie zum Fahrer?
de.question: Was sagen Sie zum Fahrer?

fr.intro: Vous êtes monté dans un taxi. Vous voulez aller à la gare. Que dites-vous au chauffeur?

options.text: À la gare, s'il vous plaît.

fr.question: Que dites-vous au chauffeur?

es.intro: Subiste a un taxi. Quieres ir a la estación. ¿Qué le dices al conductor?
es.question: ¿Qué le dices al conductor?

ko.intro: 택시를 탔습니다. 역에 가고 싶습니다. 운전사에게 뭐라고 말합니까?
ko.question: 운전사에게 뭐라고 말합니까?

meanings.tr: lütfen / rica ederim (talep kalıbı, özellikle hizmet alırken)
meanings.en: please / I request (request pattern, especially when using a service)
meanings.de: bitte (Anfragemuster, besonders bei der Nutzung eines Dienstes)
meanings.fr: s'il vous plaît (formule de demande, surtout en utilisant un service)
meanings.es: por favor (patrón de solicitud, especialmente al usar un servicio)

meanings.en: until / to (indicates going to a certain place)
meanings.de: bis (gibt an, bis wohin man fährt)

meanings.tr: Taksiye binerken gidilecek yeri belirtmek için kullanılan kalıp.
meanings.en: Pattern used in a taxi to indicate the destination.
meanings.de: Muster im Taxi, um das Ziel anzugeben.
meanings.fr: Structure utilisée dans un taxi pour indiquer la destination.
meanings.es: Patrón usado en un taxi para indicar el destino.
meanings.ko: 택시에서 목적지를 말할 때 사용하는 표현.

logic.tr: Ok karakteri taksiye yeni binen yolcudur ve şoföre nereye gideceğini söylemesi gerekmektedir. '2. えきまで　おねがいします' (İstasyona kadar lütfen.) doğrudur — taksiye binince gidilecek yeri belirten standart kalıptır. Seçenek 1 'えきまで　いきましょうか' yolcunun şoföre 'gidelim mi?' diye sorması anlamına gelir; bu ifade şoförün rolüne aittir, yoksa müşteri kendi isteğini 'おねがいします' ile belirtir (Rol Karışıklığı). Seçenek 3 'えきに　つきましたか' yolculuk bitmeden önce 'vardık mı?' diye sormaktır; araca yeni binildiğinde bu soru anlamsızdır (Bağlam Karışıklığı).
logic.en: The arrow character is the passenger who just got in the taxi and needs to tell the driver the destination. '2. えきまで　おねがいします' (To the station, please.) is correct — it is the standard pattern for telling a taxi driver where to go. Option 1 'えきまで　いきましょうか' means 'Shall we go to the station?' — this is the driver's line, not the passenger's; the passenger states a request with 'おねがいします' (Role Confusion). Option 3 'えきに　つきましたか' means 'Have we arrived at the station?' — asking this immediately after getting in makes no sense (Context Confusion).
logic.de: Der Pfeil-Charakter ist der Fahrgast, der gerade ins Taxi gestiegen ist. '2. えきまで　おねがいします' ist korrekt. Option 1 'えきまで　いきましょうか' bedeutet 'Sollen wir zum Bahnhof fahren?' — das sagt der Fahrer, nicht der Fahrgast (Rollenverwechslung). Option 3 'えきに　つきましたか' bedeutet 'Sind wir angekommen?' — direkt nach dem Einsteigen sinnlos (Kontextverwechslung).
logic.fr: Le personnage avec la flèche est le passager qui vient de monter dans le taxi. '2. えきまで　おねがいします' est correct. L'option 1 'えきまで　いきましょうか' signifie 'On va à la gare?' — c'est le chauffeur qui dirait cela (confusion de rôles). L'option 3 'えきに　つきましたか' signifie 'Sommes-nous arrivés?' — absurde juste après être monté (confusion de contexte).
logic.es: '2. えきまで　おねがいします' es correcto. La opción 1 'えきまで　いきましょうか' significa '¿Vamos a la estación?' — eso lo diría el conductor (confusión de roles). La opción 3 'えきに　つきましたか' significa '¿Llegamos?' — absurdo justo al subir (confusión de contexto).
logic.ko: 화살표 캐릭터는 택시에 막 탄 승객입니다. '2. えきまで　おねがいします'가 정답입니다. 선택지 1 'えきまで　いきましょうか'는 '역까지 갈까요?'로 운전사가 하는 말입니다(역할 혼동). 선택지 3 'えきに　つきましたか'는 '역에 도착했나요?'로 방금 탄 상황에서 할 수 없는 말입니다(상황 혼동).

image_prompt: Simple manga-style line drawing, black and white. Inside a taxi. On the LEFT, a young man with simple round head sits in the back seat, leaning slightly forward toward the driver, mouth open speaking. On the RIGHT, a taxi driver with simple round head sits in the front seat wearing a cap, looking back at the passenger. Simple car interior visible — headrests, seats. Cartoon style with simple round faces, clean outlines, white background, no shading, no color, no text, no arrows. Same style as JLPT N5 textbook manga illustrations.
